canonical_rules.operating_target: $10M is the operating target and drives all status pills. $20M stretch gaps MAY appear in the daily topline as clearly labeled columns (rule amended by Eric 2026-06-12); $20M never determines status.
canonical_rules.forecast: CRM (HubSpot) forecast is canonical for Monday standup. Live HubSpot read supersedes the sheet's HubSpot Daily Snapshot when newer.
canonical_rules.capacity_model: Top-down capacity model is recovery potential until backed by weighted CRM pipeline.
canonical_rules.closed_won_period: Q2+Q3 2026 (closedate 2026-04-01 to 2026-09-30) — verified: live CRM returns exactly $401,600 / 4 deals for this window.
canonical_rules.pipeline_scope: Operating view = Enterprise + Single Brain + Upsell (Eric, 2026-06-11). Karrot ABM is a separate labeled automation lane, never blended into status or hygiene counts.
canonical_rules.acv: Canonical ACV = $180K (Eric, 2026-06-11). Sheet assumption cell still says $100K — sheet capacity model flagged stale until updated.
canonical_rules.pace: Canonical pace = Q2+Q3 linear: target × complete-days-elapsed / 183 over 2026-04-01..2026-09-30 (Eric, 2026-06-12). Calendar-year pace is deprecated. Today: $10M pace mark $3,934,426.
canonical_rules.closed_won_headline: Deck/dashboard headline 'booked' = daily.actual_revenue (CRM Q2+Q3 closed-won, $401,600 as of Jun 12). The untraced $1.20M figure is retired (Eric, 2026-06-12).

contradictions.detail: Operating brief figures (CRM forecast $1,870,600; capacity $6,966,378; capacity-not-backed $5,095,778; gap -$8,129,400) exactly match the 2026-06-07 snapshot (git b6ca894) — the brief is a 4-day-old baseline, superseded by live CRM ($1,992,000 forecast) and the 06-10 sheet capacity model ($6,580,214).

RESOLVED 2026-06-12: definition mismatch, not deal movement. The sheet snapshot script's open-deal universe runs ~24 deals/$774K larger than hs_is_closed=false while weighted pipeline matches live CRM to the dollar (both $1,590,400 on Jun 11, both $1,743,900-consistent on Jun 12 sheet pending). Action: fix the snapshot script's open-deal filter or formally adopt the CRM definition (canonical per spec).

Sheet Actuals Log shows $0 revenue booked for week of Jun 1-7; live CRM shows one $15,000 closed-won deal. The Actuals Log is not being filled in — CRM is canonical; weekly.json now uses CRM values for closes and revenue.

Eric set canonical ACV = $180K (2026-06-11), but the sheet's SG ACV assumption cell still reads $100K ('approved ASP'). The sheet's capacity model ($6,580,214) and required-cadence outputs are stale until the cell is updated. Agents are read-only on Sheets — Eric updates the cell.

13-slide standup deck (presentation spec) shows closed-won $1.20M vs CRM $824.6K CY2026 / $401.6K Q2+Q3, 'zero deals created 6/10' vs CRM 4/$270K, and MQL/SAL/SQL lead-stage data traced to no reachable source. Deck DATA renders as 'not wired' until traced; deck LAYOUT is the approved spec.
